finding: Five representative degree-(4,2) Trans-hard limits resist every standard transcendence basis tested at dps=300: not algebraic of degree<=8, not in Q-span of {pi^k, log p, zeta_k, sqrt p, euler}, not in Q-span of elliptic/AGM constants, not in Q-span of 2F1 / Clausen / Catalan, and not of the form (algebraic)*pi or (algebraic)*pi^2. Pairwise ratios L_i/L_j are also not simple algebraic numbers. The 1162-family stratum at degree-(4,2) sits in an unidentified transcendence basis. Limits are stable to |K_2000-K_1999|<1e-300, so this is not a numerical artifact.
